claim: Conductor enforces file changes through pull request workflow rather than direct file editing to maintain code quality and review discipline
actionable: Design AI coding tools to require PR creation and approval rather than direct file writes to create enforcement checkpoints
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: Slot-free zones in codebase prevent AI from creating feedback loops of bad code by designating human-reviewed sections
actionable: Establish clear boundaries in codebase where AI cannot contribute, mark critical sections as human-only to prevent cascading code quality degradation
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: Claude Opus performs better than Claude 3.5 Sonnet for creative/exploratory coding tasks, while Sonnet is the workhorse for incremental bug fixes
actionable: Use Claude Opus for exploratory features and architectural decisions; switch to Claude 3.5 Sonnet for debugging and incremental improvements to save costs
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: Fast mode enables token maximization by reducing latency and allowing higher throughput; default mode is not optimized for token efficiency
actionable: Enable fast mode when running agents with high token budgets to maximize tokens used and reduce per-token latency costs
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: System design should not delegate UI/UX decisions to AI; human thought on layout and interaction patterns prevents generic, non-crafted interfaces
actionable: When building AI-assisted products, make architectural and UI design decisions explicitly as a human team; let AI handle implementation details only
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: Code is becoming sawdust: once models improve, old code becomes disposable; value lies in prompts and specifications instead
actionable: Shift focus from writing perfect code to writing perfect prompts and specifications; treat generated code as ephemeral and reusable only through regeneration
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: Whisper-mode microphone use reduces office distraction while enabling voice-based agent interaction, making verbal commands socially feasible
actionable: Use directional microphones when building voice-first workflows in shared office spaces to enable hands-free AI interaction without disturbing colleagues
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup

claim: Total token spend in month of $22,000 with previous-gen models; lines of code in same month was tens of thousands, prioritizing token use over code quantity
actionable: When building with agentic systems, track token spend alongside code output; recognize that token spend is the better metric than LOC for productivity
source_title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup
